generated_oml:
// C1: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Encoder measuring motor rotational position and speed for real-time state observation in MABI MAX 100 first axis"
]

instance GearboxTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Temperature sensor measuring gearbox operational temperatures for friction-induced error analysis"
]

instance MotorCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current sensor measuring motor electrical parameters for efficiency analysis and fault detection"
]

instance PositionAccuracySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Positioning sensor verifying mechanical accuracy against operational requirements"
]

instance FrictionCoefficientSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Contextual sensor measuring friction coefficients for dynamic behavior modeling"
]

// C2: Produced Data Types
instance MotorPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
]

instance GearboxTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GearboxTemperatureSensor
]

instance MotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentSensor
]

instance PositionAccuracyData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PositionAccuracySensor
]

instance FrictionCoefficientData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom FrictionCoefficientSensor
]

// C3: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction Data
instance MotorStateTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated real-time motor state trace including position, current, and derived metrics via MQTT protocol"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorPositionData, MotorCurrentData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MQTTDataAggregator
]

instance GearboxPerformanceTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Contextual gearbox performance trace aggregating temperature data with operational context"
    DTDFVocab:fromData GearboxTemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MQTTDataAggregator
]

instance PositionalAccuracyTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "High-fidelity positional accuracy trace for mechanical validation against operational requirements"
    DTDFVocab:fromData PositionAccuracyData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MQTTDataAggregator
]

instance FrictionContextTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Dynamic friction coefficient trace for modeling friction-induced errors in mechanical behavior"
    DTDFVocab:fromData FrictionCoefficientData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MQTTDataAggregator
]

// C4: Enablers/Technologies
instance MQTTDataAggregator : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol implementation for real-time low-latency data streaming from physical sensors to digital shadow"
]

instance SysMLModeler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML modeling environment for structural component and connector-based modeling of motor and gearboxes"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo StructuralModel
]

instance MATLABSimulinkEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink platform for executable simulation of dynamic behavior including friction modeling"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionSimulationModel
]

instance UMLDigitalShadowModel : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "UML-based reference model for defining one-way data flow between physical system and digital shadow"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DigitalShadowFramework
]

// C5: Models/Data Relationships
instance StructuralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML component & connector model capturing mechanical structure of motor and dual gearboxes with interfaces"
]

instance FrictionSimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink executable model simulating friction-induced errors and dynamic behavior of mechanical components"
]

instance DigitalShadowFramework : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "UML-based reference model defining data aggregation and abstraction for manufacturing-specific operational insights"
]

// C6: Services
instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time state observation service providing live motor torque, gearbox temperatures, and positional accuracy via digital shadow data aggregation"
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalInsight
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive dashboard service integrating live data streams and simulation outputs for user-friendly system behavior visualization"
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceDashboard
]

instance BehavioralAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Structural and executable simulation service enabling detailed analysis of system dynamics including friction-induced errors"
    DTDFVocab:provides DynamicAnalysisResult
]

// C7: Insights/Actions
instance OperationalInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Contextual data traces aggregating friction coefficients and load distributions for operational performance insights"
]

instance PerformanceDashboard : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive visualization of mechanical parameters (gearbox wear, motor efficiency) presented in user-friendly format for stakeholders"
]

instance DynamicAnalysisResult : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Simulation results validated against real-time physical data to ensure accuracy of friction modeling and dynamic behavior predictions"
]

// C8: Environment
instance MABIMAX100FirstAxisEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains MotorEncoder, GearboxTemperatureSensor, MotorCurrentSensor,
        PositionAccuracySensor, FrictionCoefficientSensor,
        MotorStateTrace, GearboxPerformanceTrace, PositionalAccuracyTrace,
        FrictionContextTrace, StructuralModel, FrictionSimulationModel
]

instance ManufacturingSystemEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains MonitoringService, VisualizationService, BehavioralAnalysisService,
        OperationalInsight, PerformanceDashboard, DynamicAnalysisResult,
        MQTTDataAggregator, SysMLModeler, MATLABSimulinkEngine,
        UMLDigitalShadowModel
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The provided documents do not explicitly describe a 'Digital Twin constellation' or orchestration mechanisms. However, based on the context of **Digital Shadows** and **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)**, the following inferred technical framework is grounded in the **Digital Shadow reference model** and **architecture description languages (ADLs)**:

  A **Digital Twin constellation** in this context would logically involve:
  - **Hierarchical coordination** through **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like **MontiArc** or **SysML IBDs**, which define component interconnections and behavior synchronization.
  - **Centralized vs. decentralized orchestration** could be implied by the **component & connector perspective** of ADLs, where modular systems (e.g., **ACME, AADL**) enable distributed twin instances to interact via standardized interfaces.
  - **Resource management** would align with **simulation tools** (e.g., **MathWorks Simulink**) used to model behavior in parallel with physical systems, ensuring real-time or near-real-time synchronization.
  - **System-wide coordination** could be abstracted via **state machines** (e.g., in **SysML v1.7**) or **automaton-based methods** (e.g., **MontiArc Automaton**), enabling event-driven workflows between twin instances.

  **Note:** This description is speculative due to the lack of explicit mention in the documents but aligns with broader Digital Twin principles inferred from **MBSE methodologies** and **ADLs** for complex systems."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
A Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the described context spans **model-based systems engineering (MBSE)** phases, structured through **multi-level abstraction** (requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers) as outlined in SysML and related methodologies. The lifecycle governance involves:
- **Development Phase**: Utilizes **architecture description languages (ADLs)** such as **ACME, AADL, MontiArc**, and **SysML IBDs** for component and connector modeling, complemented by **state machines** (SysML v1.7) or **MontiArc Automata** for behavior representation.
- **Representation Types**: Models are created in **heterogeneous notations** (e.g., SysML diagrams, ADL specifications) and integrated with tools like **MathWorks Simulink** or general-purpose languages (**C++/Java**) for simulation/implementation.
- **Transition Management**: Complexity is managed via **methodologies like RFLP (Requirements, Functions, Logics, Physics), OOSEM, SPES, or SMArDT**, ensuring alignment across stakeholders’ perspectives.
- **Lifecycle Governance**: Emphasizes **integration of diverse stakeholders’ views** and **multi-tool workflows** to maintain consistency across abstraction layers.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
  **Model Fidelity and Validation:**
  The DT’s fidelity is ensured through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies (e.g., RFLP, OOSEM, SMArDT) that enforce structured validation across abstraction layers. Validation relies on:
  - **Simulation tools** (e.g., MathWorks Simulink) to compare digital models with measured data traces.
  - **Uncertainty quantification** is implied via aggregation of contextual data traces, though no explicit methods (e.g., Monte Carlo simulations) or error correction mechanisms are detailed.
  Quality assurance processes are implicit in the integration of heterogeneous stakeholders’ perspectives, ensuring consistency across SysML, ADLs, and simulation outputs.
  "
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "Not in Document
"
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The provided documents do not mention **horizontal integration** with external systems, including data exchange protocols, interoperability standards, or integration patterns with other Digital Twins or enterprise systems. The focus is solely on **Digital Shadows** (one-way data flow) and **Digital Twin modeling** via MBSE methodologies (e.g., SysML, ADLs), without reference to external system dependencies or integration frameworks. Thus, this characteristic is **not in Document**."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
  The Digital Twin system adheres to **industry-specific modeling standards** through:
  - **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)**: Leverages **SysML** (Systems Modeling Language) for structured system modeling, including **Architecture Description Languages (ADLs)** like AADL or MontiArc for component-level abstraction.
  - **Digital Shadow Reference Model**: Defined by Kritzinger et al. [32], emphasizing **one-way data aggregation** (passive digital shadow) and contextual data traces, with no explicit mention of interoperability standards (e.g., OPC UA, IEC 61499).
  **Certification/Compliance**: No evidence of ISO/IEC standards (e.g., ISO/IEC 27001, IEC 62264) or certification frameworks. The system relies on **research-grade methodologies** (e.g., RFLP, SMArDT) without industry adoption validation.
  "
]
